content: what is our channel strategy and who is our biggest competitor to study right now?

# Channel Strategy Summary

**Format:** Long-form looped videos (1–4 hours) — cosmic nebula visuals + space ambient/lofi music.

**Cadence:** 1 video/week (~4/month). Quality over volume.

**Title formula:** `[Visual scene] — Deep Space Ambient for [use case]`

**Thumbnails:** Dark background, single glowing nebula, minimal text.

**Month 3+:** Push toward 4–8hr videos to capture sleep/study search traffic. Add 1 live stream/premiere per month.

**Avoid:** Posting 15+/mo (burnout pattern), generic channel names without space/cosmic/nebula keywords.

---

# Your #1 Competitor to Study: **VIATMOS**

Channel ID: `UCi0C7tOvioiAFWozysY7NyQ`

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Subs | 57,500 |
| Age | 10 months |
| Videos | 45 |
| Upload rate | 4.3/mo (~1/week) |
| Recent avg views | 86,645 |
| Top video | 423K views |

**Why they matter:** They proved the exact format works *right now*, in under a year, with the exact cadence we plan to follow. They are the live blueprint.

**What to study on their channel:**
1. **Thumbnail patterns** — which nebula compositions get clicks
2. **Title structure** — exact wording, use cases mentioned (sleep, study, meditation, deep work)
3. **Video length distribution** — are they at 1hr, 2hr, 4hr?
4. **Top 5 videos** — what scenes/themes outperformed
5. **Upload day/time** — consistency pattern
6. **Description & tag patterns** — SEO approach

---

# Secondary study target: **Relaxation Ambient Music** (456K subs, 11 yrs)

Proves the format has **decade-long longevity**. Only 1.9 uploads/mo but 137K recent avg views — evergreen library compounding. This is the long-term endgame.

---

# Counter-example (what NOT to do): **Space Relax Music Channel**

63.6K subs, 15.8 uploads/mo, but recent avg views collapsed to **1,697**. Volume without quality killed their algorithm performance. Discipline matters.

---

**Suggested next action:** Run a deep-dive on VIATMOS — pull their top 10 videos with titles, lengths, view counts, and upload dates so we can reverse-engineer the pattern. If `execution/youtube_channel_research.py` doesn't already support single-channel deep-dives, that's worth adding as a new script (e.g. `execution/youtube_channel_deepdive.py`).

Want me to draft that script spec?
